subject: Alaska.Ai HOLD (parallel run): AIDEA Mat-Su data-center deck, superseded by official No. 9 (2026-07-16)
html_body:
HOLD, do not post as the daily edition
This deck is from a routine run triggered for the 2026-07-16 slot that a session usage limit delayed into 2026-07-17. While it was paused, the official 2026-07-17 daily run already shipped and merged Carousel No. 9 ("No Road Out. Quinhagak Flies Its Own Eyes", scored 9.07) on a different story, and it already docketed this AIDEA story. So this run was NOT merged to main (that would duplicate the No. 9 number and the docket item and clobber the official ledger). Nothing on main was changed by this run.

What this is: a complete, distinct, quality deck on the AIDEA Mat-Su free-state-land data-center district (state-as-developer / land-first-purpose-later), which the official run only tracked in the docket, it did not make a deck of it. Gates: machine QA PASS 0/0; pixel + flow critics all ship; scorer 8.31 vs 8.30; vector PDF 2.3 MB, 9 slides.

Your options: (1) ship it as a bonus No. 10 (I can renumber + rebase + merge on request), or (2) discard (close PR #46). Images and PDF below are pinned to branch tsturg/ecstatic-cerf-gktbp2 (PR #46), not main.

Heads-up (already sent, not reversible): this run's docket_alerts sent a duplicate subscriber email about the same closing decisions the official run already emailed (different dedup keys, so it was not blocked).

Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 9

2026-07-16 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/ecstatic-cerf-gktbp2

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (2.3 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: First You Spend the Land. Then You Decide What For.

2 · Paste the post copy



  
3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

  
Primary reporting on the AIDEA land transfer and the Houston ban.

Editor's note

  
SHIP as a marginal pass (8.31 vs 8.3). Disclose: the plat is a deliberately restrained, honest dimetric survey aesthetic (argued in the storyboard for area honesty), not a GPU-PBR/film-graded hero, and the S4 ISOTYPE is plain flat squares. Cosmetic nits for the record: the cover USE tag string did not render on S1 (device intact on S2/S8/S9); S8 DECISION/41 pages spacing is tight. No factual, contrast, dash, emoji, hashtag, or claim-id issues; PDF vector 2.3MB; topic and hero ledger-clean.
